description:
## Component: hx-form (UPGRADE)
**Tier 2 ã Standard Enterprise | Industry Consensus: 3/6 libraries**

Upgrade existing scaffold. Light DOM form wrapper for Drupal compatibility with validation orchestration.

### ADR-001 Classification: SPECIAL ã Light DOM Component
Per our architecture, hx-form uses **Light DOM** (no Shadow DOM) so Drupal Form API tokens and hidden fields work correctly. Uses adopted stylesheets for styling. See `packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-form/hx-form.ts` for the existing pattern.

### API Design
**Properties:** `action` (form URL ã when set, renders native `<form>`), `method` (get|post), `novalidate`, `autocomplete`
**Slots:** Default (form fields ã all content from Drupal)
**Events:** `hx-submit` (composed: true, detail: { values: FormData }), `hx-invalid` (validation failed)

### Critical Drupal Pattern
```twig
{# When action is set: renders <form> tag (server-side submission) #}
<hx-form action="/patient/intake" method="post">
  {{ form.form_build_id }}
  {{ form.form_token }}
  {{ form.form_id }}
  <hx-text-input name="patient_name" required>Name</hx-text-input>
  <hx-button type="submit">Submit</hx-button>
</hx-form>

{# When no action: renders slot only (Drupal provides its own <form>) #}
{# This is the CMS-slot pattern ã Drupal's form tag wraps everything #}
<form action="/drupal-form" method="post">
  {{ form.form_build_id }}
  <hx-form>
    <hx-text-input name="field_name" required>Name</hx-text-input>
    <hx-button type="submit">Submit</hx-button>
  </hx-form>
</form>
```

The dual-mode design is critical: when Drupal provides its own `<form>` tag (standard Form API), hx-form acts as a pure styling/validation wrapper WITHOUT generating a competing `<form>` tag. This prevents nested forms.

### Healthcare: Patient intake, prescription orders, appointment scheduling

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Light DOM ã NO Shadow DOM (Drupal form tokens must be in same DOM)
2. Dual mode: generates `<form>` when action set, bare wrapper when not
3. Validates all child hx-* form elements before submission
4. Collects FormData from all ElementInternals children
5. hx-submit event with FormData for client-side handling
6. Works with Drupal Form API (CSRF tokens, form_build_id, etc.)
7. Adopted stylesheets for consistent styling without Shadow DOM
title: T2-35: hx-form ã Form Wrapper with Validation

## Component: hx-form (UPGRADE)
**Tier 2 ã Standard Enterprise | Industry Consensus: 3/6 libraries**

Upgrade existing scaffold. Light DOM form wrapper for Drupal compatibility with validation orchestration.

### ADR-001 Classification: SPECIAL ã Light DOM Component
Per our architecture, hx-form uses **Light DOM** (no Shadow DOM) so Drupal Form API tokens and hidden fields work correctly. Uses adopted stylesheets for styling. See `packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-form/hx-form.ts` for the existing pattern.

### API Design

**Slots:** Default (form fields ã all content from Drupal)
**Events:** `hx-submit` (composed: true, detail: { values: FormData }), `hx-invalid` (validation failed)

  {{ form.form_token }}
  {{ form.form_id }}

</hx-form>

{# When no action: renders slot only (Drupal provides its own <form>) #}
{# This is the CMS-slot pattern ã Drupal's form tag wraps everything #}
<form action="/drupal-form" method="post">
  {{ form.form_build_id }}
  <hx-form>
    <hx-text-input name="field_name" required>Name</hx-text-input>

The dual-mode design is critical: when Drupal provides its own `<form>` tag (standard Form API), hx-form acts as a pure styling/validation wrapper WITHOUT generating a competing `<form>` tag. This prevents nested forms.

6. Works with Drupal Form API (CSRF tokens, form_build_id, etc.)
7. Adopted stylesheets for consistent styling without Shadow DOM

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>ÞÊ Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-form/hx-form.ts`:
- Around line 83-89: Add unit tests for the new public property `autocomplete`
on the hx-form component: verify the default value is 'on', verify the attribute
reflects to the DOM when `autocomplete` is set to 'off' and back to 'on', and
verify the rendered form element includes or omits the autocomplete attribute
correctly in both cases when `action` is present and when `action` is not set
(cover attribute propagation and any conditional rendering changes introduced
with the `autocomplete` property). Locate tests for hx-form (or create a new
spec alongside existing hx-form tests), exercise the component via the public
API (set property and attribute), and assert the final rendered output contains
the expected autocomplete attribute behavior for both default and explicitly set
values.
```

</details>
